calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-15-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Hearing On Application For Entry Of Court Judgment Including Attorneys Fees, Pursuant To C.C.P. 1021.5, Against Defendant Pmc Supplies Llc Or, In The Alternative, Upon Hearing On A Date To Be Set By The Court
Off Calendar Jul-26-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:23 am and concluding at 10:05 am.

Plaintiff Keep America Safe and Beautiful is represented by Laralei Paras, Esq. via zoom video conference.
Defendant PMC supplies LLC is represented by Roberto Cruz, Esq.
Plaintiff presents summary of facts, claims and an offer of proof.

COURT FINDS:
1. The DOES in this matter were dismissed on Nov-20-2023.
2. Default entered as to the Defendant on Oct-20-2022.
3. Summons and complaint served March 4 2022
4. The Court has waived the appearance of David R Brown
4. The Court has reviewed the Declaration of David R Brown and confirmed that facts stated in it's declaration are true and correct.
5. Based on the summary of the case the notice of the Prop 65 violations was provided to Defendants
6. By proponderance of the evidence the court finds in favor of the plaintiff.

The matter is submitted.

By the statements made by Plaintiff and its counsel, documents filed, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence proving each element of the complaint.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Keep America Safe and Beautiful, and against Defendant PMC supplies LLC, as follows:

1. Damages: $2,500.00
2. Prejudgment Interest: $0
3. Costs: $1,684.18
4. Attorney fees: $32,692.53

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $36,876.71

Furthermore the Court awards Injunctive Relief as follows:

Commencing on or before the twentieth day after the Effective Date, PMC shall provide clear and reasonable warnings for all Products in accordance with this Section pursuant to Title 27, California Code of Regulations, section 25600, et seq. Each warning shall be prominently placed with such conspicuousness as compared with other words, statements, or designs as to render it likely to be read and understood by an ordinary individual under customary conditions before purchase or use and shall be provided in a manner such that it is clearly associated with the specific Product to which the warning applies. The Warning shall consist of the following statement:

WARNING: This product can expose you to di(2-ethylhexyl)phthalate (DEHP), which is known to the State of California to cause birth defects or other reproductive harm. For more information go to www.P65Warnings.ca.gov.

Proposed judgment signed and filed this date. (D505)
